conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you enquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm enquiring about my son Alex. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start by asking: **How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 19 years old, his eligibility may depend on whether he's in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or registered training?** (For example, full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is in sixth form doing his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. Now I need to understand your relationship and living arrangements with Alex.

**Do you live with Alex, and are you responsible for him?** (This means you're the one looking after him and meeting his needs)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives at home with me and I'm responsible for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

**Are you living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here?** (For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another qualifying immigration status?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm a UK citizen, so yes, I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently in hospital or care (such as local authority care)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not. He's just living at home with me and going to college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 19 years old and in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), which means he qualifies as a young person under 20 in approved education or training

✓ You live with Alex and are responsible for him

✓ You are a UK citizen living in the UK with the right to reside here

✓ Alex is not in hospital or care

✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

All the eligibility criteria are met. You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Please note that Child Benefit for young people aged 16-19 continues only as long as they remain in approved education or training, and typically stops when they turn 20 or leave education, whichever comes first.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 19 years old and is currently in approved education (doing A-levels in sixth form). According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they are in approved education or training. The claimant lives with Alex and is responsible for him, is a UK citizen living in the UK with the right to reside, Alex is not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 19-year-old son Alex. Alex qualifies because he is under 20 and in approved full-time education (A-levels). The claimant meets all the requirements: they live with and are responsible for Alex, they are a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK, Alex is not in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him.
